assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain: Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (main), Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements. Verified in task-1 (lines 1-2, 3-4, 7-8, 10-11, 13-14, 16-27, 30-34, 36-38), task-2 (lines 1-2, 4, 7-8, 10-11, 13-26, 34-39, 41-43), task-3 (lines 1-2, 4, 7-8, 10-11, 13-14, 21-33, 40-45, 47-51), task-4 (lines 1-2, 4, 7-8, 10-11, 13-21, 28-32, 34-36), task-5 (lines 1-2, 4, 7-8, 10-11, 16-34, 40-44, 46-52).

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 5 task files: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-severity-aggregation-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-integration-tests.md. 5 is between 3 and 10 inclusive.
All 5 task files have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as their first section: task-1 line 2, task-2 line 2, task-3 line 2, task-4 line 2, task-5 line 2.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency chain: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. This forms a DAG: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 5}. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_severity_summary.rs (new file in existing model/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in manifest). Task 2: Modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists in manifest). Task 3: Creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs and server/src/main.rs (both exist). Task 4: Modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (exists). Task 5: Creates tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (new file in existing tests/api/ dir), modifies tests/Cargo.toml (exists). All paths are plausible within the repo-backend.md directory tree.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map lists 8 concrete changes under trustify-backend, each naming specific files, structs, methods, endpoints, and behaviors: e.g., 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/', 'Add get_advisory_severity_summary method to SbomService in modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler', etc. None are vague or abstract.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' — a single repository name per task. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', and '## Test Requirements' sections. Verified in all task files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Modified files all appear in repo-backend.md manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, server/src/main.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml. Created files are new files in directories that exist in the manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ (new advisory_severity_summary.rs), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/ (new advisory_summary.rs), tests/api/ (new advisory_summary.rs). All parent directories exist in the manifest.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: References modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs (SbomDetails), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs (AdvisorySummary severity field). Task 2: References modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, entity::sbom, entity::sbom_advisory (entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs), entity::advisory (entity/src/advisory.rs), common/src/error.rs (AppError), common/src/db/query.rs. Task 3: References modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, common/src/error.rs, tower-http caching. Task 4: References modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/sbom/mod.rs. Task 5: References tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs, assert_eq! pattern.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Target Branch\nmain': task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map 'Description Digests' section lists all 5 tasks with digests computed by running scripts/sha256-digest.py on each task file. All digests are format-tagged 64-char lowercase hex: Task 1: sha256-md:196670169db30aa7c9792b5f3dba75e5329dc14c205701903194665ecf7ca200, Task 2: sha256-md:cbecf9d0be5259bce2367b0fc90ff3fd58c577685773ed205d2a790723bd1ea1, Task 3: sha256-md:f895045c0f751748a95f163755e3bb5123655aa753d1faa1bc8e6f4a33d44765, Task 4: sha256-md:909f26fec39c15b429ba827effa2ed3d5682dd7c744fb6b9b1162e535c92a3ec, Task 5: sha256-md:8361f2ec453cf2de6a4dd1023ff1a2095bfe691b2c1af36a75426025f750fc55. All use the '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' format.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: The repo-backend.md fixture lists a CONVENTIONS.md file in the trustify-backend repo root, but no CONVENTIONS.md content was provided as an eval fixture. The task descriptions do not include any convention-aware enrichment (no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references or 'Applies:' rationale lines in Implementation Notes). Since the repo manifest indicates CONVENTIONS.md exists, convention enrichment should have been attempted. However, without the actual CONVENTIONS.md content being available as a fixture file, conventions could not be read or applied. The assertion requires validation of convention applicability, which was not performed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9001 has Priority: Major (not 'Undefined'). All 5 task files include an 'additional_fields' section with 'priority: Major': task-1 line 41, task-2 line 50, task-3 line 57, task-4 line 42, task-5 line 58.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9001 has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (non-empty). CLAUDE.md does not have a '### Jira Field Defaults' section with fixVersion scope, so it defaults to 'both'. All 5 task files include 'fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0' in their additional_fields section: task-1 line 42, task-2 line 51, task-3 line 58, task-4 line 43, task-5 line 59.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Summary Comment (Feature TC-9001 — Step 6c)' section states: 'Priority: Major — inherited from feature TC-9001, set on all 5 tasks via additional_fields.' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from feature TC-9001, set on all 5 tasks via additional_fields (fixVersion scope defaults to both per configuration).' Both inherited values are explicitly documented.
